question: Why use sliding window attention? What is the trade-off?
code_hint: Reduces compute from O(n^2) to O(n*w), limits receptive field to window size
expected_output: Trade-off: O(n*w) compute vs O(n^2), local context only, often combined with global attention
template:
# Sliding Window Attention:
# - Limits attention to window_size context
# - Complexity: O(n * window_size) instead of O(n^2)
# - Captures local patterns, loses long-range dependencies
# - Often combined with global attention for key tokens

print("Trade-off: O(n*w) compute vs O(n^2), local context only, often combined with global attention")
answer:
# Sliding Window Attention:
# - Limits attention to window_size context
# - Complexity: O(n * window_size) instead of O(n^2)
# - Captures local patterns, loses long-range dependencies
# - Often combined with global attention for key tokens

print("Trade-off: O(n*w) compute vs O(n^2), local context only, often combined with global attention")
